response: CSRD (Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive, 2022/2464/EU) is the EU law that requires in-scope companies to publish sustainability disclosures in the management report using ESRS standards. Sources: CSRD Art.1 https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX%3A32022L2464 | CSRD Art.3 https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX%3A32022L2464

query: Does CSRD apply to a company with 600 employees?
response: Likely yes. Companies above 500 employees are within the first CSRD application wave, and scope is expanded through phased implementation. Sources: CSRD Art.1 https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX%3A32022L2464 | CSRD Art.10 https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX%3A32022L2464

query: What is due diligence under CSRD?
response: Under CSRD, due diligence reporting means describing sustainability risk identification, mitigation, and value-chain impacts, including supplier-related adverse impacts. Sources: CSRD Art.8 https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX%3A32022L2464

query: What are Article 8 and 9 in SFDR?
response: SFDR distinguishes products that promote ESG characteristics (Article 8) from products with a sustainable investment objective (Article 9), each with different disclosure obligations. Source: SFDR https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX%3A32019R2088

query: Do we need a roadmap for CSRD compliance?
response: Yes. Start with scoping and double materiality, then data controls and assurance readiness, then digital-tagged reporting workflow. Sources: CSRD Art.3 https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX%3A32022L2464 | CSRD Art.4 https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX%3A32022L2464
